evals.prompt: I need a skill for building AI-powered search that uses DSPy retrievers and rerankers. None of the existing skills cover this. Can you help me create one?
evals.expected_output: Confirms the gap exists, checks for close matches like ai-searching-docs, then asks whether to build or request the skill. If building, creates SKILL.md with proper frontmatter (no quoted description), ai- prefix naming, and submits a PR via gh pr create.

assertions.name: checks_existing_skills
assertions.description: Verifies whether an existing skill already covers the use case before proceeding

assertions.name: offers_build_or_request
assertions.description: Presents the two paths (build via PR or request via issue) for the user to choose

assertions.name: uses_ai_prefix
assertions.description: Names the new skill with ai- prefix (problem-first) not dspy- prefix

assertions.name: plain_yaml_description
assertions.description: The generated SKILL.md description uses plain YAML scalars — no quotes wrapping the value

assertions.name: submits_to_github
assertions.description: Actually runs gh pr create or gh issue create — does not just describe what to do

evals.prompt: There should be a skill for AI-powered code review with DSPy. I don't have time to build it myself — can you just file an issue?
evals.expected_output: Files a GitHub issue on lebsral/DSPy-Programming-not-prompting-LMs-skills with a structured body including the problem description, relevant DSPy capabilities, example use case, and suggested trigger phrases. Returns the issue URL.

assertions.name: uses_gh_issue_create
assertions.description: Runs gh issue create command rather than just drafting the issue

assertions.name: includes_trigger_phrases
assertions.description: Issue body includes suggested trigger phrases for the skill description
